description: This figure is a correlation scatter matrix for Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel systems, analyzing pairwise relationships between 12 variables: alloying elements (C, Si, Mn, Cr, S, Ni, P, Fe, Mo, V, W, all measured in wt.%) and Vickers microhardness (in HV). Diagonal subplots present univariate histograms showing the distribution of each variable across the studied dataset, including three arc-cladded experimental steel samples and supplementary compiled alloy data. Off-diagonal subplots are pairwise scatter plots to reveal correlation patterns: carbide-forming elements (C, Cr, Mo, W) show clear positive correlations with microhardness, while Fe content exhibits a negative correlation with hardness. The matrix quantifies feature relationships to support machine learning model training for high-accuracy steel property prediction, confirming direct composition-performance correlations that underpin the subsequent solid solution strengthening analysis and alloy design workflow.
